title: ceratostylis clavata
intro: Ceratostylis clavata is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, club-shaped inflorescence. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Ceratostylis clavata are said to include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as treatment for skin conditions and wounds. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in infusions, decoctions, or combined with other herbs to create remedies for various ailments.
